title: Deschampsia neumaniana
intro: Deschampsia neumaniana, also known as Neumann's hair grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Deschampsia neumaniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Deschampsia neumaniana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are used in tinctures, infusions, and salves for various health applications.
taxonomy: Deschampsia neumaniana belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a species of grass within the genus Deschampsia. The genus Deschampsia is classified under the tribe Poeae and subfamily Pooideae. Deschampsia neumaniana is native to South America and is a perennial grass species. This species is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, and class Liliopsida.
